title: Partition Labels
problem_statement: Partition a string into the maximum number of segments such that each letter appears in at most one segment.
constraints: s consists of lowercase English letters.
intuition: Use the last occurrence of each character to extend the current partition until it can safely end.

approaches.name: Greedy partition by last occurrence
approaches.description: Record the last index of every character, then scan and grow the current partition until the current position reaches the furthest last index in the segment.
approaches.why_not: Trying all partitions is exponential; greedy based on last occurrences produces the maximum number of valid partitions.

interview_qa.q: Why track the last occurrence of each character?
interview_qa.a: It ensures that a partition does not end before all instances of a character are included.
No, because extending the partition to the furthest last occurrence is always safe and maximizes the number of segments.
